mnemonics.title: Framing Methods
mnemonics.content: Character uses CHARacters (DLE), Bit uses BITs (0 after five 1s)

mnemonics.title: ARQ Protocols
mnemonics.content: Go-Back-N: GO back and repeat ALL | Selective Repeat: SELECTIVE - only repeat what's broken

mnemonics.title: HDLC Frame Fields
mnemonics.content: FAGS CAFE - Flag, Address, Control, Frame check, Everything (Data)

mnemonics.title: Flow Control
mnemonics.content: STOP and WAIT = One at a time | SLIDING WINDOW = Many in transit

acronyms.usage: Error control mechanism that automatically retransmits corrupted frames
Polynomial-based error detection method used in data transmission
Physical address of network interface card (48-bit)
Protocol for direct connection between two nodes
Error detection field in data link frames (CRC)
Signal indicating frame was not received correctly

visualTips.visualization: Think of '1's as a chain of 6 connected links - you must insert a '0' (spacer) after every 5 links to break the pattern before reaching the flag (6th link)
Imagine a window sliding over sequence numbers like a conveyor belt - frames enter from right (send), exit from left (ACK received)
Positions 1,2,4,8,16... are parity bits (powers of 2). All other positions are data bits. Each parity bit checks specific bit positions based on binary representation
Go-Back-N = throw away everything after error and restart | Selective Repeat = keep what's good, fix only what's broken

analogies.analogy: Like sending letters one by one and waiting for reply before sending the next - reliable but slow for bulk mail
Like a restaurant with multiple table reservations - you can seat several groups while waiting for earlier ones to finish
Like adding a pause in speech when saying 'um' to prevent breaking up a word into something that sounds like the code word
Like a mathematical fingerprint - dividing data by a special number and keeping the remainder as proof the data wasn't changed
